text.title: C# Background Services Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce a worker spec + worker class + DI registration + xUnit test.

step.name: classify-job-shape
step.action: Apply decision tree: durability needed? throughput? CPU-heavy? distributed? If durability=must-survive OR CPU=heavy → STOP and route to Hangfire / Worker host.
step.decision-gate: If decision != in-process BackgroundService, STOP and apply the right component's methodology.

step.name: design-channel
step.action: If queue is needed: `Channel.CreateBounded<T>(capacity, FullMode=Wait)`. Otherwise `PeriodicTimer` for periodic work or direct EF query loop for poll-based.

step.name: write-worker-class
step.action: Class extends `BackgroundService`. Constructor: `IServiceProvider`, channel, `ILogger<T>`. `ExecuteAsync` loops `await foreach (var item in channel.Reader.ReadAllAsync(stoppingToken))` with `try/catch` and `CreateScope` per item.

step.name: register-and-healthcheck
step.action: In `Program.cs`: register channel as singleton, register `IHealthCheck` tracking last-success timestamp, `builder.Services.AddHostedService<T>()`, `AddHealthChecks().AddCheck<Health>("worker-name")`.

step.name: xunit-test
step.action: xUnit test: instantiate `Worker`, write to channel, call `StartAsync`, wait for handler, `StopAsync`. Assert stoppingToken triggers loop exit.
step.decision-gate: If worker doesn't exit within 1s of `StopAsync`, the stoppingToken propagation rule is violated.
